stimulus:
[Bar graph: Additional Solar Energy Generated When Sunlight Hours Exceeded Forecast (in thousands of MWh). Two months shown.]

  • July: +120 thousand MWh above forecast
  • August: −90 thousand MWh below forecast (less sunlight than predicted)

During July, weather conditions were ______
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to complete the example?

answerOptions.content: so cloudy that the electricity from solar panels was about 90 thousand MWh less than predicted.
sunny enough to generate about 120 thousand more MWh of electricity from solar panels.
sunny enough to generate about 90 thousand more MWh of electricity from solar panels.
so cloudy that the electricity from solar panels was about 120 thousand MWh less than predicted.

rationale:
Choice B is correct because the graph shows July generated about 120 thousand MWh more than predicted, indicating sunnier-than-expected conditions.

Choice A is incorrect: −90 thousand MWh corresponds to August, not July. Choice C is incorrect: 120 thousand MWh was the surplus in July, not 90. Choice D is incorrect: July was a surplus (+120), not a shortfall.
